articles.title: 3 priorities for aerospace aluminium recycling in 2026
articles.summary: In a Novelis-authored opinion piece, the company outlines three priorities for aerospace aluminium recycling in 2026: improving scrap traceability, with five Airbus facilities having launched a pilot programme; closing the aerospace supply chain loop; and scaling remelting capacity ahead of accelerating fleet retirement, projected to rise from approximately 700 to 1,100 aircraft per year by 2030.

articles.title: Aluminium giant Constellium's FY25 profit gains 358%: would CBAM cloud the outlook?
articles.summary: Constellium's FY2025 results showed net income rising 358% to $275 million (from $60 million in FY2024), adjusted EBITDA up 36% to $846 million, and revenue growing 15% to $8.4 billion; the company simultaneously launched "Vision 2028" targets of $900 million EBITDA and $300 million annual free cash flow by 2028, with analysts noting that the EU's broadening CBAM scope could add cost pressure to import-exposed operations.
articles.novelis_angle: Constellium's sharp earnings recovery and Vision 2028's $900M EBITDA target signal growing competitive firepower in FRP at a time when Novelis is absorbing Bay Minette greenfield capex and Oswego restart costs.

articles.title: Reframing scrap: why the UK must recognise aluminium as a strategic resource
articles.summary: An AlCircle analysis argues the UK government should formally classify aluminium scrap as a strategic resource, citing secondary aluminium's up to 95% energy savings versus primary production and its contribution to UK net-zero targets; the article calls for scrap export restrictions and public investment in domestic remelting capacity.
articles.novelis_angle: UK recognition of aluminium scrap as a strategic resource could reduce export competition and improve domestic scrap availability for Novelis's Latchford recycling plant, currently undergoing its £90M capacity expansion for beverage can recycling.
